behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: A Unit test with synthetic diff data could miss GitHub's actual diff format quirks (merge diffs, multiple files, context handling). Integration with a realistic GitHub diff format catches real diff parsing failures that mock-only tests would hide.
behaviors.test_description: Parse a realistic GitHub diff structure containing multiple files and context lines. Verify that only the lines with +/- markers are extracted for analysis and that the full file content is not included. Use GitHub API response format to ensure real diff parsing rules are applied.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: GitHub's position numbering for diff-view comments uses specific rules that a Unit test with synthetic positions could violate. Integration mocking with realistic GitHub API position formats (accounting for merge diffs, deleted code, multiple hunks) catches position calculation errors that pure logic tests miss.
behaviors.test_description: Mock GitHub's diff API with realistic response including multiple hunks, deleted lines, and context. Calculate comment positions for identified findings. Verify that calculated positions match expected GitHub line positions in the diff view format, accounting for GitHub's specific numbering rules.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: A mocked LLM response will always return whatever we program it to. Only a real LLM call can demonstrate whether the model can actually identify real defects. This requires the real Claude API, not a mock, to test actual code understanding capability.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real Python code sample with a known introduced bug (e.g., unchecked null dereference, logic error, missing error handling) to the actual Claude API. Verify that the LLM response identifies the specific defect by name or description. Run multiple times if needed to account for LLM variability.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: This is a data consistency invariant: given a set of findings and inline comments created from those findings, the summary must match. No external I/O or LLM unpredictability is involved—this is pure logic verification of the summary aggregation algorithm.
behaviors.test_description: Generate a set of findings (e.g., 5 bugs at specific lines). Create inline comments for each finding. Generate the summary comment from the same findings list. Verify that every finding in the summary appears exactly once and corresponds to a created inline comment with matching line number and finding text.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This requires: (1) real GitHub API calls for diff retrieval and comment posting (System), (2) real LLM calls to analyze code (Agentic), and (3) correct orchestration of multiple agentic steps in sequence. Only Workflow level tests catch failures in the coordination and real-world timing of these dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real GitHub pull request with a known bug, execute the full automation: fetch the diff from GitHub, pass changed lines to the actual Claude API, parse the response for findings, post inline comments to the PR via real GitHub API, post the summary comment to the PR, then verify all comments are visible on GitHub and the summary matches the inline comments.
